sn.s: Im Lade gseh ich öpper, wo verlore uussieht.
sn.e: In the shop I see someone who looks lost.

sn.s: Er probiert z'rede, aber d'Wörter chömed nöd.
sn.e: He tries to speak, but the words won't come.

sn.s: D'Verchäuferin verstaht en nöd, und er wird root.
sn.e: The shopkeeper doesn't understand him, and he turns red.

sn.s: Ich kenne das Gfühl nu z'guet.
sn.e: I know that feeling all too well.

sn.s: "Cha ich hälfe?" frag ich en, uf Änglisch.
sn.e: "Can I help?" I ask him, in English.

sn.s: Ich übersetze und erklär em, was er säge mues.
sn.e: I translate and explain to him what he needs to say.

sn.s: "Säg eifach Grüezi, das isch de Aafang," säg ich.
sn.e: "Just say Grüezi, that's the start," I say.

sn.s: "Perfekt! Gsehsch, du chasch's," säg ich ufmunternd.
sn.e: "Perfect! See, you can do it," I say encouragingly.

sn.s: Vor es paar Jahr bin ich gnau a sinere Stell gsii.
sn.e: A few years ago I was in exactly his place.

sn.s: Jetz cha ich ihm gä, was mer öpper gä het.
sn.e: Now I can give him what someone once gave me.

sn.s: "Es wird besser, versproche," säg ich zum Schluss.
sn.e: "It gets better, I promise," I say at the end.

sn.s: De Chreis schliesst sich, und das fühlt sich guet aa.
sn.e: The circle closes, and that feels good.
